/* Section */
.himex-content {
    color: #fff;
}

.himex-badge {
    display: inline-block;
    border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.25);
    border-radius: 40px;
    padding: 10px 24px;
    margin-bottom: 30px;
    font-size: 15px;
    font-weight: 600;
    letter-spacing: 1px;
}

.himex-badge-label {
    display: inline-flex;
    align-items: center;
    gap: 6px;
    font-size: 12px;
    font-weight: 600;
    color: #111;
    background: #f3f4f6;
    padding: 5px 10px;
    border-radius: 999px;
    line-height: 1;
}

.himex-badge-icon {
    width: 14px;
    height: 14px;
    flex-shrink: 0;
    color: #111; /* controls SVG stroke */
}

.himex-content h3 {
    font-size:36px;
  font-weight:700;
  text-transform:uppercase;
    line-height:48px;
    margin-bottom: 30px;
    color: #fff;
}

.himex-content p {
    font-size: 16px;
    line-height: 1.6;
    margin-bottom:30px;
    color: rgba(255,255,255,.9);
}

.himex-list {
    list-style: none;
    padding: 0;
}

.himex-list li {
    position: relative;
    padding-left: 30px;
    margin-bottom: 20px;
    font-size: 15px;
    color: #fff;
}

.himex-list li:before {
    content: "";
    width: 10px;
    height: 10px;
    background: #2ecc71;
    border-radius: 50%;
    position: absolute;
    left: 0;
    top: 12px;
}

/* Form Card */

.himex-form-card{
    background:#f4f4f4;
    padding:50px;
    border-radius:16px;
    box-shadow:0 15px 40px rgba(0,0,0,.12);
}

.himex-form-card h3{
    text-align:center;
    font-size:36px;
  font-weight:700;
  text-transform:uppercase;
  line-height:48px;
    margin-bottom:25px;
    color:#0d2d5c;
}

.himex-form-card .subtitle{
    text-align:center;
    color:#7b8794;
    margin-bottom:35px;
}

.himex-form-card label{
    display:block;
    margin-bottom:10px;
    font-weight:600;
    color:#5b6470;
}

.himex-form-card input[type=email]{
    width:100%;
    height:64px;
    border:1px solid #d7dde5;
    border-radius:8px;
    padding:0 20px;
    margin-bottom:20px;
}

.checkbox-row{
    display:flex;
    align-items:flex-start;
    gap:10px;
    margin-bottom:25px;
}

.checkbox-row input{
    margin-top: 8px;
}

.himex-form-card input[type=submit]{
    width:100%;
    height:64px;
    border:none;
    border-radius:8px;
    background:#01cb68;
    color:#fff;
    font-weight:700; 
  text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:18px;
    cursor:pointer;
}

.himex-form-card input[type=submit]:hover{
    opacity:.9;
}

.download-note{
    margin-top:25px;
    color:#97a0aa;
}

/* Mobile */

@media(max-width:768px){

    .himex-content h3 {
        font-size: 28px;
    }

    .himex-content p {
        font-size: 15px;
    }

    .himex-list li {
        font-size: 15px;
    }

    .himex-form-card {
        padding: 30px;
        margin-top: 30px;
    }
}

/* Section */
.himex-content {
    color: #fff;
}

.himex-badge {
    display: inline-block;
    border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.25);
    border-radius: 40px;
    padding: 10px 24px;
    margin-bottom: 30px;
    font-size: 15px;
    font-weight: 600;
    letter-spacing: 1px;
}

.himex-badge-label {
    display: inline-flex;
    align-items: center;
    gap: 6px;
    font-size: 12px;
    font-weight: 600;
    color: #111;
    background: #f3f4f6;
    padding: 5px 10px;
    border-radius: 999px;
    line-height: 1;
}

.himex-badge-icon {
    width: 14px;
    height: 14px;
    flex-shrink: 0;
    color: #111; /* controls SVG stroke */
}

.himex-content h3 {
    font-size:36px;
        font-weight:700;
        text-transform:uppercase;
    line-height:48px;
    margin-bottom: 30px;
    color: #fff;
}

.himex-content p {
    font-size: 16px;
    line-height: 1.6;
    margin-bottom:30px;
    color: rgba(255,255,255,.9);
}

.himex-list {
    list-style: none;
    padding: 0;
}

.himex-list li {
    position: relative;
    padding-left: 30px;
    margin-bottom: 20px;
    font-size: 15px;
    color: #fff;
}

.himex-list li:before {
    content: "";
    width: 10px;
    height: 10px;
    background: #2ecc71;
    border-radius: 50%;
    position: absolute;
    left: 0;
    top: 12px;
}

/* Form Card */

.himex-form-card{
    background:#f4f4f4;
    padding:50px;
    border-radius:16px;
    box-shadow:0 15px 40px rgba(0,0,0,.12);
}

.himex-form-card h3{
    text-align:center;
    font-size:36px;
        font-weight:700;
        text-transform:uppercase;
        line-height:48px;
    margin-bottom:25px;
    color:#0d2d5c;
}

.himex-form-card .subtitle{
    text-align:center;
    color:#7b8794;
    margin-bottom:35px;
}

.himex-form-card label{
    display:block;
    margin-bottom:10px;
    font-weight:600;
    color:#5b6470;
}

.himex-form-card input[type=email]{
    width:100%;
    height:64px;
    border:1px solid #d7dde5;
    border-radius:8px;
    padding:0 20px;
    margin-bottom:20px;
}

.checkbox-row{
    display:flex;
    align-items:flex-start;
    gap:10px;
    margin-bottom:25px;
}

.checkbox-row input{
    margin-top: 8px;
}

.himex-form-card input[type=submit]{
    width:100%;
    height:64px;
    border:none;
    border-radius:8px;
    background:#01cb68;
    color:#fff;
    font-weight:700; 
        text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:18px;
    cursor:pointer;
}

.himex-form-card input[type=submit]:hover{
    opacity:.9;
}

.download-note{
    margin-top:25px;
    color:#97a0aa;
}

/* Mobile */

@media(max-width:768px){

    .himex-content h3 {
        font-size: 28px;
    }

    .himex-content p {
        font-size: 15px;
    }

    .himex-list li {
        font-size: 15px;
    }

    .himex-form-card {
        padding: 30px;
        margin-top: 30px;
    }
}


/* =========================
   HIMALAYAN JOURNAL SECTION
=========================== */

.himex-journal-section{
    padding:120px 40px;
    background:#f8f6f2;
}

.himex-journal-header{
    text-align:center;
    max-width:800px;
    margin:0 auto 70px;
}

.himex-journal-pill{
    display:inline-block;
    padding:10px 24px;
    border-radius:50px;
    background:#edf2eb;
    color:#0b3d2e;
    font-size:18px;
    font-weight:400;
        line-height:27px;
    letter-spacing:1px;
    text-transform:uppercase;
}

.himex-journal-header h3{
    margin:25px 0;
    font-size:36px;
    font-weight:700;
    line-height:47px;
    text-transform:uppercase;
    color:#0f2f61;
}

.himex-journal-header p{
    font-size:18px;
        font-weight:400;
    color:#666;
    line-height:27px;
}

.himex-journal-grid{
    max-width:1140px;
    margin:auto;
    display:grid;
    grid-template-columns:1.7fr 1fr;
    gap:30px;
}

.himex-side{
    display:grid;
    gap:30px;
}

.himex-card{
    position:relative;
    overflow:hidden;
    border-radius:18px;
    min-height:320px;
}

.featured{
    min-height:700px;
}

.himex-card img{
    width:100%;
    height:100%;
    object-fit:cover;
    position:absolute;
    inset:0;
    transition:transform .8s ease;
}

.himex-card:hover img{
    transform:scale(1.08);
}

.himex-overlay{
    position:absolute;
    inset:0;
    background:linear-gradient(
        to top,
        rgba(6,20,40,.92),
        rgba(6,20,40,.15)
    );
}

.himex-journal-content{
    position:absolute;
    bottom:40px;
    left:40px;
    right:40px;
    z-index:2;
}

.himex-category{
    display:inline-block;
    padding:8px 18px;
    background:#1d8f57;
    color:#fff;
    border-radius:30px;
    font-size:12px;
    font-weight:700;
    letter-spacing:1px;
    margin-bottom:20px;
}

.himex-journal-content h3{
    color:#fff;
    font-size:24px;
    line-height:1.2;
    margin-bottom:20px;
}

.himex-card:not(.featured) h3{
    font-size:24px;
}

.himex-journal-content p{
        color:rgba(255,255,255,.85);
    margin-bottom:25px;
    line-height:24px; 
        font-size:15px; 
        font-weight:400;
}

.himex-journal-content a{
    color:#4ade80;
    font-weight:700;
    text-decoration:none;
}

.himex-journal-footer{
    text-align:center;
    margin-top:60px;
}

.himex-journal-btn{
    display:inline-block;
    background:#01cb68;
    color:#fff;
    padding:18px 40px;
    border-radius:8px;
    text-decoration:none;
    text-transform:uppercase;
        font-weight:700;
    transition:.3s;
}

.himex-journal-btn:hover{
    transform:translateY(-4px);
}

/* ===========================
   LARGE DESKTOPS
=========================== */

@media (min-width:1800px){

    .himex-journal-grid{
        max-width:1700px;
    }

    .featured{
        min-height:850px;
    }

    .himex-journal-content h3{
        font-size:42px;
    }
}

/* =========================
   LAPTOPS & SMALL DESKTOPS
=========================== */

@media (max-width:1200px){

    .himex-journal-section{
        padding:100px 30px;
    }

    .featured{
        min-height:600px;
    }

    .himex-journal-content{
        left:30px;
        right:30px;
        bottom:30px;
    }

    .himex-journal-content h3{
        font-size:32px;
    }
}

/* ========================
   TABLETS
=========================== */

@media (max-width:991px){

    .himex-journal-section{
        padding:90px 25px;
    }

    .himex-journal-grid{
        grid-template-columns:1fr;
    }

    .featured{
        min-height:550px;
    }

    .himex-journal-header{
        margin-bottom:50px;
    }

    .himex-journal-header h3{
        font-size:34px;
    }

    .himex-journal-header p{
        font-size:18px;
    }

    .himex-journal-content h3{
        font-size:30px;
    }

    .himex-card:not(.featured) h3{
        font-size:24px;
    }
}

/* =========================
   MOBILE DEVICES
=========================== */

@media (max-width:767px){

    .himex-journal-section{
        padding:70px 20px;
    }

    .himex-journal-header{
        margin-bottom:40px;
    }

    .himex-journal-header h3{
        font-size:28px;
        line-height:1.2;
    }

    .himex-journal-header p{
        font-size:16px;
        line-height:1.7;
    }

    .featured{
        min-height:420px;
    }

    .himex-card{
        min-height:300px;
        border-radius:14px;
    }

    .himex-journal-content{
        left:22px;
        right:22px;
        bottom:22px;
    }

    .himex-journal-content h3{
        font-size:24px;
        margin-bottom:15px;
    }

    .himex-card:not(.featured) h3{
        font-size:22px;
    }

    .himex-journal-content p{
        font-size:14px;
        line-height:1.6;
        margin-bottom:18px;
    }

    .himex-category{
        font-size:11px;
        padding:7px 14px;
        margin-bottom:14px;
    }

    .himex-journal-btn{
        width:100%;
        max-width:320px;
        text-align:center;
        padding:16px 25px;
    }
}

/* =======================
   SMALL MOBILE
========================== */

@media (max-width:480px){

    .himex-journal-section{
        padding:60px 15px;
    }

    .featured{
        min-height:360px;
    }

    .himex-card{
        min-height:260px;
    }

    .himex-journal-content{
        left:18px;
        right:18px;
        bottom:18px;
    }

    .himex-journal-content h3{
        font-size:20px;
    }

    .himex-card:not(.featured) h3{
        font-size:18px;
    }

    .himex-journal-content p{
        display:none;
    }

    .himex-journal-header h3{
        font-size:24px;
    }

    .himex-journal-header p{
        font-size:15px;
    }
}

Group Size</div> </div> </div><div class="tours-tabs__info__item"> <div class="tours-tabs__info__item__content"> <div class="tours-tabs__info__item__icon"><i class="td-mountains"></i></div> <div class="tours-tabs__info__item__title">5545 m</div> <div class="tours-tabs__info__item__description">Max-altitude</div> </div> </div></div> <div class="tours-tabs__content padding-all"> <ul class="tour-categories-list list-block list-block--tour-tabs"><li><a href="https://www.himalayanexploration.com/tour-category/deals-and-discounts/">Deals and Discounts</a></li><li><a href="https://www.himalayanexploration.com/tour-category/everest-region/">Everest Region</a></li><li><a href="https://www.himalayanexploration.com/tour-category/nepal/">Nepal - Land of Himalaya</a></li><li><a href="https://www.himalayanexploration.com/tour-category/trekking-holiday/">Trekking Holiday</a></li></ul><h2 style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude="">Join the Adventure of a Lifetime with the Everest Base Camp Trek</h2> <p style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude="">If you have ever dreamed of standing at the foot of the world’s tallest mountain, you can experience the awe-inspiring views of the Everest region for yourself. The incredible views will captivate you. <strong>Trekking to Everest Base Camp</strong> is a seriously epic adventure – far beyond the easy hiking.</p> <p style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude="">You’ve probably spent hours scrolling over photos of the mountain and the rest of the surrounding landscape, and wondering what it would be like actually to be there in person.</p> <p style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude="">But then reality sets in. You wonder if you’re capable of doing something that epic.</p> <p style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude="">The Everest Base Camp Trek is one of those adventures that changes everything. I’m talking about breathtaking Himalayan views that’ll make you forget to breathe, hanging out with the Sherpa people who’ll become your heroes, and standing at the base of <strong>Mount Everest</strong>–yeah, that Everest. Whether you’ve never done a serious hike in your life or you’re already a seasoned mountain climber, this 14-day journey works for everyone, from <strong>total beginners to mountain veterans, older adults, and families</strong> who want their kids to experience what real adventure looks like.</p> <p style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude="">At <strong>Himalayan Exploration Treks</strong>, we get it. We’ve been where you are, staring at that 65 km journey, wondering if we could pull it off. That’s why we focus on keeping you safe through proper acclimatization, showcasing the authentic culture, and ensuring you don’t feel like just another tourist.</p> <hr class="he-divider"> <h2 style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude="" class="nitro-offscreen">Package Highlights</h2> <div class="row nitro-offscreen"><div class="col-md-6">You’ll reach <strong><a href="https://www.himalayanexploration.com/south-base-camp-in-nepal/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Everest South Base Camp</a> (5,364 m)</strong> — and trust me, it hits differently when you’re there</div><div class="col-md-6">You get to experience<strong> genuine Sherpa hospitality</strong> – these people will blow your mind with their kindness</div></div><br> <div class="row nitro-offscreen"><div class="col-md-6">You’re trekking through <strong>Sagarmatha National Park</strong> – it’s UNESCO-protected for a reason<br> </div><div class="col-md-6">We’ve figured out the perfect balance of <strong>challenging days and rest days</strong> so you don’t burn out</div></div><br> <div class="row nitro-offscreen"><div class="col-md-6">The Khumbu region ecosystem is unlike anything you’ve ever experienced</div><div class="col-md-6">The <strong>panoramic views</strong> of Everest, Lhotse, Nuptse & <a href="https://www.himalayanexploration.com/tours/ama-dablam-expedition/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Ama Dablam</a> will ruin every other mountain view for you</div></div><br> <div class="row nitro-offscreen"><div class="col-md-6"> <strong>EBC Trek at a Glance</strong> <table class="table table-bordered tours-tabs__table"> <tbody> <tr> <td><strong>Trek Duration:</strong></td> <td>14 days</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Max. Altitude:</strong></td> <td>5545 meters</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Trek Difficulty: </strong></td> <td>Strenuous</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Trek Starts/Ends:</strong></td> <td>Lukla/Lukla</td> </tr> <tr> <td><strong>Accommodation:</strong></td> <td>Tea houses/Lodges</td> </tr> </tbody> </table> </div><div class="col-md-6"><a href="https://www.google.com/maps/d/embed?mid=11wWw2wSViL3YwbneBB8cQiwPARE" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"><strong>Everest Base Camp Trek Map</strong></a> <div style="height: 420px; width: 100%; display: inline-block; overflow: hidden;" nitro-exclude=""><iframe style="position: relative; top: -67px; border: none;" title="Everest Base Camp Trek Map" width="100%" height="440" nitro-exclude="" allow="autoplay; encrypted-media; gyroscope;" nitro-lazy-src="https://www.google.com/maps/d/embed?mid=11wWw2wSViL3YwbneBB8cQiwPARE" class="custom-google-map nitro-lazy"></iframe></div> </div></div> <hr class="he-divider nitro-offscreen"> <div class="short-itinerary section nitro-offscreen"> <h2 class="heading">Everest Base Camp 14-day itinerary</h2> <div class="short-itinerary__items"> <div class="short-itinerary__item"> <h3><span class="short-itinerary__day">Day 01: Arrive in Kathmandu (1,350 m)</span></h3> <p>You arrive at Tribhuvan International Airport in Kathmandu (1,400 m/4,593 ft), and we escort you to your hotel. You will stay overnight at a comfortable 3-star hotel.</p> </div> <div class="short-itinerary__item"> <h3><span class="short-itinerary__day">Day 02: Fly to Lukla (2840 m) and trek to Phakding 2600 m)</span></h3> <p>Here’s where it gets real – you take a 35-minute flight from Kathmandu airport to Lukla (2,840 m/9,318 ft). Alternatively, if flights aren’t available, you can drive 5- 6 hours to Manthali/Ramechhap (474 m/1,555 ft) and then take a quick 20-minute <a href="https://www.himalayanexploration.com/guide-to-flying-to-lukla/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">flight to Lukla</a>. Then you start walking to Phakding (2,610 m/8,563 ft) – about 3 to 4 hours of your first real trekking. You’ll sleep in a mountain lodge.</p> </div> <div class="short-itinerary__item"> <h3><span class="short-itinerary__day">Day 03: Trek to Namche Bazaar (3,440 m)</span></h3> <p>This is where your legs start figuring out what you’ve gotten yourself into. Phakding to Namche Bazaar (3,440 m/11,286 ft) takes about 5 to 6 hours—another lodge night.</p> </div> <div class="short-itinerary__item"> <h3><span class="short-itinerary__day">Day 04: Acclimatization day! Hike to Everest View Hotel (3,800 m)</span></h3> <p>Acclimatization day – you stay put in Namche Bazaar but hike up to Hotel Everest View (3,880 m/12,730 ft). It’s 4 to 5 hours total at high altitude, but you come back down to sleep. Your body will thank you later.</p> </div> <div class="short-itinerary__item"> <h3><span class="short-itinerary__day">Day 05: Trek to Tengboche (3,800 m)</span></h3> <p>Namche Bazaar to Tengboche (3,860 m/12,664 ft) – another 5 to 6-hour day. The monastery here is something else.</p> </div> <div class="short-itinerary__item"> <h3><span class="short-itinerary__day">Day 06: Trek to Dingboche (4,400 m)</span></h3> <p>Keep pushing from Tengboche to Dingboche (4,410 m/14,469 ft). Yeah, it’s another 5 to 6-hour day, but you’re getting stronger.</p> </div> <div class="short-itinerary__item"> <h3><span class="short-itinerary__day">Day 07: Acclimatization Day and hike to Nangkartshang Peak (5,083 m)</span></h3> <p>Another rest and acclimatization day. You hike up to Nangkartsang Peak (5,083 m/16,676 ft) – about 4 to 5 hours – but come back to the same guesthouse.</p> </div> <div class="short-itinerary__item"> <h3><span class="short-itinerary__day">Day 08: Trek to Lobuche (4,900 m)</span></h3> <p>Dingboche to Lobuche (4,910 m/16,109 ft). It’s getting thinner up here due to the cold, and you’ll feel it for the next 5 to 6 hours.</p> </div> <div class="short-itinerary__item"> <h3><span class="short-itinerary__day">Day 09: Trek to Gorak Shep (5100 m), then visit EBC (5,364 m), and then back.</span></h3> <p>The big day. You finally reach <strong>Everest South Base Camp</strong> (5,364 m/17,598 ft) and then head to Gorak Shep (5,190 m/17,028 ft). It is a long 8- to 9-hour day, but you did it.</p> </div> <div class="short-itinerary__item"> <h3><span class="short-itinerary__day">Day 10: Climb to Kala Patthar (5,555 m) and then descend to Panboche (3,900 m)</span></h3> <p>If you thought yesterday was big, wait until you see the sunrise from Kala Patthar (5,555 m/18,225 ft). Then you hike down to Pheriche (4,240 m/13,911 ft). It’s about 7 to 8 hours, but it’s mostly downhill.</p> </div> <div class="short-itinerary__item"> <h3><span class="short-itinerary__day">Day 11: Trek to Namche Bazaar (3,440 m)</span></h3> <p>Back to Namche Bazaar (3,440 m/11,286 ft) – roughly 7 to 8 hours. You’ll be amazed at how different it feels going down.</p> </div> <div class="short-itinerary__item"> <h3><span class="short-itinerary__day">Day 12: Trek to Lukla (2800 m)</span></h3> <p>Final push back to Lukla (2,840 m/9,318 ft). It’s another 7 to 8-hour day, but you’re practically walking down now.</p> </div> <div class="short-itinerary__item"> <h3><span class="short-itinerary__day">Day 13: Fly back to Kathmandu (1,350 m)</span></h3> <p>You fly back to Kathmandu (35 minutes) or catch that 20-minute flight to Manthali/Ramechhap, then drive 5 to 6 hours back to the city. Back to your 3-star hotel.</p> </div> <div class="short-itinerary__item"> <h3><span class="short-itinerary__day">Day 14: Final Departure: </span></h3> <p>Congratulations, you made it! Transfer to the Airport for your flight home.</p> </div> <blockquote> <p style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude=""><em>Want the complete, detailed PDF? Just ask, and we’ll send it over</em>. If you prefer expanding the experience, check out our package <a href="https://www.himalayanexploration.com/tours/everest-base-camp-trek-with-helicopter-return/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><strong>Everest Base Camp with fly back Helicopter</strong></a>.</p> </blockquote> <hr class="he-divider"> <h2 style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude="">Who Is This Trek For?</h2> <p>Look, I’ve seen all kinds of people crush this trek. Here’s who we’re talking about:</p> <ul style="list-style-type: square;" nitro-exclude=""> <li><strong>Never hiked before?</strong> No problem. We go slow, we acclimatize properly, and we don’t let you fail.</li> <li><strong>Already experienced adventurers?</strong> You’ll love the challenge, the altitude, and the crazy, diverse landscapes.</li> <li><strong>Getting older, but still got fire?</strong> We pace things right and provide porter assistance so you can focus on the experience.</li> <li><strong>Want to bring your teenagers?</strong> Perfect. They’ll learn more in two weeks than in a whole semester of school.</li> </ul> <hr class="he-divider"> <h2 style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude="">Packing Essentials for the Everest Base Camp Hike</h2> <p style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude="">You don’t want to be that person who packed the wrong things. Here’s what actually matters:</p> <ul style="list-style-type: square;" nitro-exclude=""> <li style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude="">Boots that don’t hurt your feet (break them in first, seriously)</li> <li style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude="">Layers on layers on layers (base, fleece, down, shell – trust the system)</li> <li style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude="">Sun protection (the UV up there will fry you)</li> <li style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude="">Water bottle & purification tabs</li> <li style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude="">Your own first-aid stuff</li> </ul> <blockquote> <p style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude=""><em>Need the complete packing list? We’ve got a detailed one that works</em>.</p> </blockquote> <hr class="he-divider"> <h2 style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude="">Best Times for Hiking to Everest Base Camp</h2> <ul style="list-style-type: square;" nitro-exclude=""> <li style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude=""><strong>Autumn (September–November)</strong> – Crystal-clear views, perfect conditions; this is the peak season for good reason</li> <li style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude=""><strong>Spring (March-May)</strong> – Everything’s blooming, especially those insane rhododendrons; the weather’s stable, and trails look like something out of a movie</li> </ul> <blockquote> <p style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude="">Winter and monsoon? Yes, it’s harder, but with way fewer people and a different experience if you’re up for it.</p> </blockquote> <hr class="he-divider"> <h2><i class="fa-solid fa-shield-plus" style="color: #74c0fc;" nitro-exclude=""></i>Safety & Altitude Acclimatization</h2> <p style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude="">Here’s the thing about altitude – it doesn’t care how fit you are, and it can lead to acute mountain sickness if you’re not careful.</p> <p style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude="">I’ve seen marathon runners struggle while grandparents cruise right through. That’s why we build on rest days and follow the <strong>“climb high, sleep low”</strong> rule. Our guides monitor you closely and recognize the signs of altitude sickness.</p> <p style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude=""><em>But the real secret? </em>Drink water like it’s your job, eat even when you don’t want to, and sleep when we tell you to sleep. It’s not glamorous, but it works.</p> <blockquote> <p style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude=""><em>Emergency helicopter evacuation is available (but you need insurance to use it)</em>.</p> </blockquote> <hr class="he-divider"> <h2><i class="fa-solid fa-leafy-green"></i>Sustainable Trekking with Himalayan Exploration Treks</h2> <p style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude="">The <a href="https://www.himalayanexploration.com/tour-category/everest-region/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Everest region</a> is fragile. Like, really fragile.</p> <p style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude="">We’re not just passing through—we live here, work here, and care about what happens after you leave. That’s why we practice <strong>eco-conscious trekking</strong>, minimize waste, utilize sustainable resources, and ensure that local communities benefit from your visit.</p> <p style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude="">You’re not just a tourist checking off a bucket list item. You’re part of protecting something incredible for the next person who dreams of standing where you stand.</p> <hr class="he-divider"> <h2>Why Trek Everest Base Camp with Himalayan Exploration Treks?</h2> <ul style="list-style-type: square;" nitro-exclude=""> <li><i class="fa fa-check icon-tick icon-tick--on"></i>Our guides have been doing this longer than most people have been dreaming about it</li> <li><i class="fa fa-check icon-tick icon-tick--on"></i>We planned the acclimatization schedule based on what works, not what looks good on paper</li> <li><i class="fa fa-check icon-tick icon-tick--on"></i>We help you plan everything before you even get here</li> <li><i class="fa fa-check icon-tick icon-tick--on"></i>Small groups mean we know your name</li> <li><i class="fa fa-check icon-tick icon-tick--on"></i>We give a damn about the environment</li> </ul> <hr class="he-divider"> <h2>Ready to Go on Your Everest Base Camp Trek?</h2> <p style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude="">Look, you’ve been thinking about this for a while now.</p> <p style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude="">It may have been years. Maybe you’ve been making excuses or waiting for the “right time.”</p> <p style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude=""><em>But here’s what I know</em> – there’s never going to be a perfect time. Your legs will never feel ready enough, your bank account will never feel full enough, and your calendar will never feel clear enough.</p> <p style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude="">The <strong>Everest Base Camp Trek</strong> with <strong>Himalayan Exploration Treks</strong> isn’t just about reaching Base Camp. It is about proving to yourself that you can.</p> <p style="text-align: justify;" nitro-exclude=""><strong>Reserve Your Trek Today</strong><br> <a href="https://www.himalayanexploration.com/contact-us/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Contact Us for Availability & Custom Dates</a><br> <a href="https://www.himalayanexploration.com/everest-base-camp-trek-a-complete-guide-for-ebc-trek/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Check it Out: Complete Guide</a></p> <p><strong>Himalayan Exploration – The journey on Foot together</strong></p> </div> </div> </div> </div> <div class="tab-pane fade nitro-offscreen" id="tabatab0"> <div class="tours-tabs__content padding-all"> <div class="timeline"><div class="timeline__item"> <div class="timeline__item__icon-wrap"> <div class="timeline__item__icon"> <div class="timeline__item__icon__text">1</div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item__content padding-left"> <h3 class="timeline__item__title">Day 1: Arrival in Kathmandu.</h3> <div class="timeline__item__description">Arrival at the airport in Kathmandu. Welcome by the agency representative at the airport and transfer to your hotel.</div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item"> <div class="timeline__item__icon-wrap"> <div class="timeline__item__icon"> <div class="timeline__item__icon__text">2</div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item__content padding-left"> <h3 class="timeline__item__title">Day 2: Free day in Kathmandu</h3> <div class="timeline__item__description">Trek preparation and sightseeing heritage site of Kathmandu.</div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item"> <div class="timeline__item__icon-wrap"> <div class="timeline__item__icon"> <div class="timeline__item__icon__text">3</div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item__content padding-left"> <h3 class="timeline__item__title">Day 3: Kathmandu –Lukla (2800m) – Phakding(2600m) 40 minutes by plane and 3h00 walk. </h3> <div class="timeline__item__description">The adventurous journey to Everest Base Camp in the early morning transfer to the domestic terminal Flight to Lukla, the get way of Everest region known as Khumbu. Wonderful opportunities to see the chain Himalayas in a 40-minute flight from Kathmandu to Lukla. Then a short trek to Phakding. </div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item"> <div class="timeline__item__icon-wrap"> <div class="timeline__item__icon"> <div class="timeline__item__icon__text">4</div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item__content padding-left"> <h3 class="timeline__item__title">Day 4: Trek to Namche Bazaar. (3440 m) 6h00 walk.</h3> <div class="timeline__item__description">On the second of the journey, the trails lead you to Namche bazaar, bypassing several Sherpa villages and encountering smiling people, their vibrant cultures, landscapes, and forests. The trails ascend through the forests and bridges until the Dudh Koshi and Bhote Koshi meet point. About 2 hours steep climb, there is the first approach of Mt. Everest, then continues to walk to Namche Bazaar, the land of Sherpa.</div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item"> <div class="timeline__item__icon-wrap"> <div class="timeline__item__icon"> <div class="timeline__item__icon__text">5</div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item__content padding-left"> <h3 class="timeline__item__title">Day 5: Acclimatization day, explore the village.</h3> <div class="timeline__item__description">The unique village of Sherpa, Namche Bazaar, can get modern amenities like the internet, ATMs, Shops, restaurants, and bakery-cafes. Namche Bazaar is considered a center point of the lower and upper Everest (Khumbu) region and has Government offices for local administration. Day trip to Syangboche (Everest View hotel) in the morning, where we can see Mt. Everest and other major peaks of the Khumbu region and give proper acclimatization.</div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item"> <div class="timeline__item__icon-wrap"> <div class="timeline__item__icon"> <div class="timeline__item__icon__text">6</div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item__content padding-left"> <h3 class="timeline__item__title">Day 6: Namche – Tengboche (3860m)</h3> <div class="timeline__item__description">It is as usual after breakfast in Namche, trek to the next destination Tengboche, a large monastery of the region where the Mani Rimdu festival conduct in October and many monks are meditation along the year. Enjoy the magnificent view of the thunder Himalayas. The trail regularly goes down to reach Sansa, where we confluence a junction to go popular trail to Gokyo valley and Everest Base camp. Gradually descend to the Dudh Koshi River traversing villages and pine forests, and cross a bridge slightly uphill similar to Tengboche.</div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item"> <div class="timeline__item__icon-wrap"> <div class="timeline__item__icon"> <div class="timeline__item__icon__text">7</div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item__content padding-left"> <h3 class="timeline__item__title">Day 7: Tengboche - Dingboche (4410m)</h3> <div class="timeline__item__description">]A picturesque visit surrounds the Monastery and Himalayan scenery in the morning, as usual, starts the day’s journey toward Dingboche. An easy walk day, even at high altitude, passes through several villages, Mani walls, and chhorten, influenced by Tibetan Buddhism. Entering to Imja-Tse valley spectacular views of the mountain remains from Dingboche</div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item"> <div class="timeline__item__icon-wrap"> <div class="timeline__item__icon"> <div class="timeline__item__icon__text">8</div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item__content padding-left"> <h3 class="timeline__item__title">Day 8: Acclimatization day!</h3> <div class="timeline__item__description">Acclimatization day! Exploring the village and climbing up to a viewpoint of 5,000 m gives us proper acclimatization. From the viewpoint, it offers spectacular mountain scenery, including Lhotse (8,500 m), Island peak, Lobuche peak, Amadablam, and other major trekking peaks. Dingboche retains its beautiful fields and pasture in Imja Valley.</div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item"> <div class="timeline__item__icon-wrap"> <div class="timeline__item__icon"> <div class="timeline__item__icon__text">9</div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item__content padding-left"> <h3 class="timeline__item__title">Day 9: Dingboche – Labuche (4900m) 5h00 walk</h3> <div class="timeline__item__description">An adventurous journey to Everest base camp from Dingboche remains in Himalayan views in 360° along the trail to Lobuche. The trail seems easy to walk due to the altitude and difficulty breathing during the walk. On a steep walk through the moraine of Khumbu glacier from Thukla to Lobuche, we encounter many prayer flags and monuments in memory of the mountaineers who deceased during the expedition.</div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item"> <div class="timeline__item__icon-wrap"> <div class="timeline__item__icon"> <div class="timeline__item__icon__text">10</div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item__content padding-left"> <h3 class="timeline__item__title">Day 10: Lobuche – Everest Base Camp (5364m) – Gorakshep (5140m) 7h00 walk.</h3> <div class="timeline__item__description">The trail goes to Gorakshep along the edge of the Khumbu glacier. The vistas of snowy mountains we still perceive. A small climb of Changri glacier and the zigzag trail gets Gorakshep. After having lunch in Gorakshep, heading to Everest Base Camp, where we can see numerous tents in the Basecamp for expedition purposes. Your dream comes true while reaching Everest Base camp, but Everest is not visible here. Then back to Gorakshep.</div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item"> <div class="timeline__item__icon-wrap"> <div class="timeline__item__icon"> <div class="timeline__item__icon__text">11</div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item__content padding-left"> <h3 class="timeline__item__title">Day 11: Gorakshep – Kalapattar (5545m) – Pangboche (3900m) 7h00 walk.</h3> <div class="timeline__item__description">We achieved the ultimate goal of the trip. Here begins another climax of the trek to reach the top of Kala Patthar, where we can see a stunning view of Mt. Everest so far. Then continue to descend to Panboche with similar views of thunder Himalayas.</div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item"> <div class="timeline__item__icon-wrap"> <div class="timeline__item__icon"> <div class="timeline__item__icon__text">12</div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item__content padding-left"> <h3 class="timeline__item__title">Day 12: Pangboche – Namche 6h00 walk</h3> <div class="timeline__item__description">Easy walk to Dibuche, then climb up to Tengboche. Descending to Pungki thanka, climb up to Sanasa, where the trail junction goes to Gokyo Lake, Khumjung village, and Namche. Afterward, easy walk to Namche; the same views remain while ascending.</div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item"> <div class="timeline__item__icon-wrap"> <div class="timeline__item__icon"> <div class="timeline__item__icon__text">13</div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item__content padding-left"> <h3 class="timeline__item__title">Day 13: Namche – Lukla 7h00 walk.</h3> <div class="timeline__item__description">The first part of the trail is steep, descending up to River and passing the same things we have seen while ascending.</div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item"> <div class="timeline__item__icon-wrap"> <div class="timeline__item__icon"> <div class="timeline__item__icon__text">14</div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item__content padding-left"> <h3 class="timeline__item__title">Day 14: Lukla – Kathmandu (Hotel includes)</h3> <div class="timeline__item__description">Early morning, 40-minute thrilling flights to an airstrip at Lukla by private plane offers a marvelous view of Central Himalayas and as well as beautiful landscape and hills.</div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item"> <div class="timeline__item__title">Day 15: Free day in Kathmandu (Hotel includes)</h3> <div class="timeline__item__description">The day can be occupied as security day if the plane doesn’t fly in case of bad weather.</div> </div> </div> <div class="timeline__item"> <div class="timeline__item__icon-wrap"> <div class="timeline__item__icon"> <div class="timeline__item__icon__text">16</div> </div> </div> But you can also use a purifier or SteriPen. It is expensive to buy water (maybe ten times the cost of purchasing water in Kathmandu).</div> </div> </div> <div class="panel panel-default accordion__item"> <div class="panel-heading accordion__item__title-wrap"> <h4 class="panel-title"> <a data-toggle="collapse" data-parent="#ataccordion1" href="#ataccordion1_2" class="accordion__item__link collapsed">Am I going to be sick on my trek at altitude?</a> </h4> </div> <div id="ataccordion1_2" class="panel-collapse collapse"> <div class="panel-body accordion__item__content">Be conscious. This trek will be over 3500 m in elevation. The possibilities of having an altitude illness usually begin at this altitude. But to prevent this, we have planned the itinerary to give you time to acclimatize as much as possible.</div> </div> </div> <div class="panel panel-default accordion__item"> <div class="panel-heading accordion__item__title-wrap"> <h4 class="panel-title"> <a data-toggle="collapse" data-parent="#ataccordion1" href="#ataccordion1_3" class="accordion__item__link collapsed">How can I conquer the altitude sickness?</a> </h4> </div> <div id="ataccordion1_3" class="panel-collapse collapse"> <div class="panel-body accordion__item__content">There’s nobody who’s going to suffer from altitude sickness. The younger and fitter people do not experience high altitude illnesses when they feel older and less fit. It’s not related to fitness or age. Speak to your private practitioner. Prescription medicines are available to help avoid the symptoms of altitude sickness. But go gently and listen to the body and be mindful of any changes and listen to your guide if he thinks that you have symptoms (which might not be apparent).</div> </div> </div> <div class="panel panel-default accordion__item"> <div class="panel-heading accordion__item__title-wrap"> <h4 class="panel-title"> <a data-toggle="collapse" data-parent="#ataccordion1" href="#ataccordion1_4" class="accordion__item__link collapsed">What happens if an emergency evacuation is required while I am trekking?</a> </h4> </div> <div id="ataccordion1_4" class="panel-collapse collapse"> <div class="panel-body accordion__item__content">We will be able to collect your travel insurance policies and details before your trip. In case of emergency, we will be able to coordinate with your insurance company and helicopters company for emergency evacuation. </div> </div> </div> <div class="panel panel-default accordion__item"> <div class="panel-heading accordion__item__title-wrap"> <h4 class="panel-title"> <a data-toggle="collapse" data-parent="#ataccordion1" href="#ataccordion1_5" class="accordion__item__link collapsed">What about simple medication during the trek?</a> </h4> </div> <div id="ataccordion1_5" class="panel-collapse collapse"> <div class="panel-body accordion__item__content">There is no doubt only be a First aid. We take a first-aid kit on every trip. We always advise you to make your own. Here’s a checklist of items you can bring, recommended. Vaccinations are not compulsory in Nepal; however, we suggest that you keep this checklist if necessary.</div> </div> </div> <div class="panel panel-default accordion__item"> <div class="panel-heading accordion__item__title-wrap"> <h4 class="panel-title"> <a data-toggle="collapse" data-parent="#ataccordion1" href="#ataccordion1_6" class="accordion__item__link collapsed">Do I need to buy travel insurance before coming to Nepal?</a> </h4> </div> <div id="ataccordion1_6" class="panel-collapse collapse"> <div class="panel-body accordion__item__content">Yes, we only provide insurance to our staff. We recommend that you purchase insurance in your home country. Note: Insurance should cover medical and emergency evacuation.</div> </div> </div> <div class="panel panel-default accordion__item"> <div class="panel-heading accordion__item__title-wrap"> <h4 class="panel-title"> <a data-toggle="collapse" data-parent="#ataccordion1" href="#ataccordion1_7" class="accordion__item__link collapsed">Should I use crampons for this trek?</a> </h4> </div> <div id="ataccordion1_7" class="panel-collapse collapse"> <div class="panel-body accordion__item__content">No. The trails are well-known. You will not need crampons if you are not traveling in the mid-winter. Instead of the crampons, bring non-technical, lightweight spikes, are less costly and suit any shoe size.</div> </div> </div> <div class="panel panel-default accordion__item"> <div class="panel-heading accordion__item__title-wrap"> <h4 class="panel-title"> <a data-toggle="collapse" data-parent="#ataccordion1" href="#ataccordion1_8" class="accordion__item__link collapsed">Will tents and mattresses use for the trek?</a> </h4> </div> <div id="ataccordion1_8" class="panel-collapse collapse"> <div class="panel-body accordion__item__content">No, we do not need tents or mattresses. There are tea houses along the trek.</div> </div> </div> <div class="panel panel-default accordion__item"> <div class="panel-heading accordion__item__title-wrap"> <h4 class="panel-title"> <a data-toggle="collapse" data-parent="#ataccordion1" href="#ataccordion1_9" class="accordion__item__link collapsed">What happens if I forget to bring something home with me?</a> </h4> </div> <div id="ataccordion1_9" class="panel-collapse collapse"> <div class="panel-body accordion__item__content">When you forget something, it may be complicated to find something here. Check here for a detailed packing list for (general) trekking in Nepal. If you’re not a regular hiker, you can rent most of Nepal’s trekking equipment upon your arrival.</div> </div> </div> <div class="panel panel-default accordion__item"> <div class="panel-heading accordion__item__title-wrap"> <h4 class="panel-title"> <a data-toggle="collapse" data-parent="#ataccordion1" href="#ataccordion1_10" class="accordion__item__link collapsed">Can I leave my stuff in Kathmandu while I am on trek?</a> </h4> </div> <div id="ataccordion1_10" class="panel-collapse collapse"> <div class="panel-body accordion__item__content">Anything you do not want for the trip can leave at our office or your hotel. We recommend that you make sure your bag is lockable.</div> </div> </div> <div class="panel panel-default accordion__item"> <div class="panel-heading accordion__item__title-wrap"> <h4 class="panel-title"> <a data-toggle="collapse" data-parent="#ataccordion1" href="#ataccordion1_11" class="accordion__item__link collapsed">Who will be the guide?</a> </h4> </div> <div id="ataccordion1_11" class="panel-collapse collapse"> <div class="panel-body accordion__item__content">Generally, we use local guides. Our guides have been numerous times on each trek to the relevant areas. All are qualified, competent, versatile, and certified licensed guides with the Government of Nepal.</div> </div> </div> <div class="panel panel-default accordion__item"> <div class="panel-heading accordion__item__title-wrap"> <h4 class="panel-title"> <a data-toggle="collapse" data-parent="#ataccordion1" href="#ataccordion1_12" class="accordion__item__link collapsed">Do I need a porter for my trek?</a> </h4> </div> <div id="ataccordion1_12" class="panel-collapse collapse"> <div class="panel-body accordion__item__content">If you use a porter, there are two advantages:<br> 1. You can enjoy your trek entirely without carrying a heavy backpack.<br> 2. Another is to increase the local communities’ income.</div> </div> </div> <div class="panel panel-default accordion__item"> <div class="panel-heading accordion__item__title-wrap"> <h4 class="panel-title"> <a data-toggle="collapse" data-parent="#ataccordion1" href="#ataccordion1_13" class="accordion__item__link collapsed">How many Kilograms can a porter carry?</a> </h4> </div> <div id="ataccordion1_13" class="panel-collapse collapse"> <div class="panel-body accordion__item__content">A porter can carry an average of 20 kg with his or her bag so that they can carry up to 16 kg of your equipment and gears. But we provide a backpack for them. </div> </div> </div> <div class="panel panel-default accordion__item"> <div class="panel-heading accordion__item__title-wrap"> <h4 class="panel-title"> <a data-toggle="collapse" data-parent="#ataccordion1" href="#ataccordion1_14" class="accordion__item__link collapsed">Are your staff insured against accidents? </a> </h4> </div> <div id="ataccordion1_14" class="panel-collapse collapse"> <div class="panel-body accordion__item__content">Yes, of course. itemprop="description" content="Join the Adventure of a Lifetime with the Everest Base Camp Trek If you have ever dreamed of standing at the foot of the world's tallest mountain, you can experience the awe-inspiring views of the Everest region for yourself. The incredible views will captivate you. Trekking to Everest Base Camp is a seriously epic adventure - far beyond the easy hiking. You've probably spent hours scrolling over photos of the mountain and the rest of the surrounding landscape, and wondering what it would be like actually to be there in person. But then reality sets in. You wonder if you're capable of doing something that epic. The Everest Base Camp Trek is one of those adventures that changes everything. I'm talking about breathtaking Himalayan views that'll make you forget to breathe, hanging out with the Sherpa people who'll become your heroes, and standing at the base of Mount Everest–yeah, that Everest. Whether you've never done a serious hike in your life or you're already a seasoned mountain climber, this 14-day journey works for everyone, from total beginners to mountain veterans, older adults, and families who want their kids to experience what real adventure looks like. At Himalayan Exploration Treks, we get it. We've been where you are, staring at that 65 km journey, wondering if we could pull it off. That's why we focus on keeping you safe through proper acclimatization, showcasing the authentic culture, and ensuring you don't feel like just another tourist. Package Highlights Everest Base Camp 14-day itinerary Day 01: Arrive in Kathmandu (1,350 m) You arrive at Tribhuvan International Airport in Kathmandu (1,400 m/4,593 ft), and we escort you to your hotel. You will stay overnight at a comfortable 3-star hotel. .expert-container {

.expert-title {

  font-size: 22px;
  margin-bottom: 15px;
}

.expert-images {
  display: flex;
  justify-content: center;
  align-items: center;
  position: relative;
  margin-bottom: 12px;
}

.expert-images img {
  border-radius: 50%;
  border: 3px solid #D0D0D0;
  width: 55px;
  height: 55px;
  object-fit: cover;
  transition: transform 0.3s ease, z-index 0.3s ease, box-shadow 0.3s ease;
}

.expert-images img:not(:first-child) {
  margin-left: -18px;
}

.expert-images img.main {
  width: 85px;
  height: 85px;
  z-index: 3;
}

.expert-images img:hover {
  transform: scale(1.1);
  z-index: 5;
  box-shadow: 0 4px 8px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.2);
}

.expert-name {
  font-weight: 600;
  margin: 6px 0 0;
}

.expert-country,
.expert-whatsapp {
  margin: 0;
}

.expert-whatsapp a {
  color: #007b55;
  font-weight: 600;
  text-decoration: none;
}

.expert-whatsapp a:hover {
  text-decoration: underline;
}

/* ---------- RESPONSIVE DESIGN ---------- */
@media (max-width: 600px) {
  .expert-container {
    padding: 30px 10px;
  }
  .expert-images img {
    width: 45px;
    height: 45px;
  }
  .expert-images img.main {
    width: 70px;
    height: 70px;
  }
  .expert-images img:not(:first-child) {
    margin-left: -10px;
  }
  .expert-title {
    font-size: 20px;
  }
}
